html:
Haohai Biotec: a messy reset with real upside

Haohai Biotec is not the clean sub-6x earnings story in the old draft. The 2025 numbers were ugly: revenue fell 8.7% to RMB2.45 billion, reported shareholder profit fell 40% to RMB251 million, and 1Q26 revenue and net profit fell another 7.6% and 18.6%. The current H-share price is about HKD20.88 as of 18 May 2026, putting the company near HKD4.7 billion of equity value, or roughly USD600 million.

The stock is still interesting because the bad year looks like a reset, not structural decay. The 2025 annual report shows RMB151 million of goodwill impairment plus RMB25 million of brand impairment tied mainly to imported IOL assets and expected volume-based procurement pressure. That depressed reported earnings, but operating cash flow was still RMB491 million and capex was RMB184 million, leaving roughly RMB307 million of free cash flow. Cash and bank balances were RMB2.45 billion against RMB399 million of borrowings. On a forward normalized basis, the H-share is not optically dirt cheap on reported P/E, but it is cheap on enterprise value to recoverable cash earnings.

The variant perception is that Haohai is being priced as a broken ophthalmology and low-end filler company just as the old profit pools have been written down and the business mix is changing. Medical aesthetics and wound care were still 42% of 2025 revenue, even after a weak year. The headline filler line fell 23%, but the weakness was concentrated in first and second generation entry-level HA fillers and a VAT change at Shanghai Qisheng. The offset is more interesting: high-end Hyalumatrix MoonWhite, approved in July 2024, is already contributing incremental revenue, the company’s first lidocaine HA filler was approved in December 2025, and the pipeline includes temporal depression, intimate-use filler, collagen products, HA skin boosters, and calcium hydroxyapatite microsphere fillers.

The overlooked part is that this is not only a filler story. Domestic EndyMed Pro gold RF microneedling is growing quickly, with 2025 domestic device revenue up 33.5% and consumable needle revenue up 56.6%, while hEGF “Healin” grew 7.9% and holds a top-two domestic share. Anti-adhesion and hemostasis grew 57.6%, helped by Kangrui Gel porcine fibrin sealant. Ophthalmology is under pressure, but Haohai still has the largest China OVD share and owns global optometry materials assets through Contamac. This complexity is why the stock screens badly, but it also means 2025 did not kill the platform.

Capital allocation is the main reason the H-share can work. Haohai keeps buying H shares in a market with only about 36 million H shares outstanding. It repurchased 277,100 H shares on 27 April, 180,600 on 28 April, 85,200 on 6 May, and 70,000 on 7 May. The company also proposed an RMB0.60 final dividend for 2025, on top of the RMB0.40 interim dividend, so the trailing gross yield is roughly 5% at today’s price. A new 10% H-share buyback mandate is on the 29 May 2026 meeting agenda. If management keeps retiring H shares near book value and below intrinsic value, the thin float can become a feature, not just a liquidity penalty.

The biggest risk is governance. Jiang Wei, one of the controlling shareholders and de facto controllers, was penalized by the CSRC for insider trading and was later released on bail pending trial in relation to that matter. The company says the matter is unrelated to Haohai, that Jiang holds no director or executive role, and that operations are normal. That may be true operationally, but the discount is deserved until the case is resolved. The governance overhang is probably why the H-share can trade near 0.8x book and at a steep discount to the A-share.

The base case is not heroic. Assume 2026 remains soft, with revenue around RMB2.35 billion to RMB2.45 billion and net profit around RMB320 million to RMB360 million as IOL pressure continues but 2025 impairments do not repeat. Then assume 2027 improves to RMB400 million to RMB450 million as high-end filler, EndyMed China, Healin, hemostasis, and new approvals offset low-end HA and procurement pressure. At HKD20.88, the market cap is about HKD4.7 billion and enterprise value is only about HKD2.3 billion to HKD2.5 billion after net cash. That is roughly 5x to 6x normalized free cash flow if the company returns toward RMB400 million of annual cash earnings.

This is a re-rating setup, not a clean compounder. The bull case needs three things: governance noise stops getting worse, H-share buybacks continue, and 2H26 or 2027 earnings show that 2025 was a kitchen-sink reset. If those happen, the market can stop valuing Haohai as a melting hospital-consumables supplier and value it as a cash-rich aesthetics, ophthalmology, wound-care, and regenerative biomaterials platform with mid-teens normalized earnings growth potential.

Bear case: HKD14, about 33% downside. Low-end HA keeps shrinking, IOL procurement cuts are worse than expected, governance remains a permanent discount, and normalized earnings settle near RMB200 million to RMB250 million. Net cash and book value prevent a total collapse, but the stock trades as a value trap.

Base case: HKD32, about 53% upside. Earnings recover toward RMB400 million to RMB450 million by 2027, buybacks continue, the dividend remains around 4% to 5%, and the H-share trades at roughly 14x to 16x normalized earnings, still discounted for governance and liquidity.

Bull case: HKD50, about 139% upside. Hyalumatrix MoonWhite, lidocaine fillers, RF microneedling consumables, hEGF, Kangrui Gel, and regenerative materials drive a visible earnings rebound above RMB500 million, while H-share buybacks shrink the float and the A-H discount compresses. That would still not require a premium medical-aesthetics multiple, only a move from “broken China device stock” to “recovering cash-rich biomaterials platform.”
